title: Global Culture Resistance and Reforms
description: This exercise will focus on the effects of globalization and international organizations using the themes of CDI and GOV and addressing learning objectives F through I. It will have 3 types based on the three AP topics/lessons, scaled x4 (total 12).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Widespread use of internet applications like YouTube is best used as an example of which of the following historical developments in the twentieth and twenty-first centuries?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: consumer culture became more global
travel infrastructure improved global mobility
the public sphere emerged
people got their information from mass media

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies an impact of social media on politics in the twenty-first century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was used to coordinate movements like the Arab Spring.
It led to an increase in the number of voters.
It facilitated rebellions against the Chinese government in Tiananmen Square.
It created a forum that could be easily regulated by totalitarian governments.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies a major cause of mass migrations in the latter half of the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: decolonization
the Cold War
the rise of multinational corporations
climate change

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following twenty-first century developments is best illustrated by the production and sale of the iPhone?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer culture transcended national borders.
Technology advanced faster than production.
New trade routes facilitated access to diverse goods.
Social changes were brought on by increased internet access.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments could a historian use to support a criticism of free trade practices?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: environmental degradation brought on by industrial factories
political destabilization brought on by mass migrations for seasonal labor
supply chain issues after a global pandemic
increased tariffs and trade barriers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Activists working to change or abolish the World Trade Organization were responding to which of the following broad historical trends in the late-twentieth and early-twenty-first centuries?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: economic globalization
the emergence capitalism
mercantilism
fair trade practices

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Monica Ghosh: How and why did you get involved in the WTO mobilizations? 
>
>Tyree Scott: Well, for years now, [the Labor and Employment Law Office] has been involved with workers outside the United States, and in the last few years, we’ve come to understand the importance of this question of trade. And so we’ve actually, unlike a lot of the groups that came together just around this international stuff or the trade stuff or because of the WTO, we were already doing work around international trade and the relationship between workers in our country and workers abroad…
>
>In 1997, we had a meeting right here in Washington at Seabeck,1 over on the peninsula. That meeting was about bringing ordinary workers together to talk about this question of trade and how we related to it, and what it meant for workers in our country and workers abroad. What solidarity would mean, and what the current policies of both our government, the government of the G-7 countries, and the international lending institutions, what their policies represented. 
>
>So we were 35 workers from 11 countries. That’s what the discussion was about, like, how do we join this discussion about something as important as trade that affects our lives. 

Source: Interview with Tyree Scott, World Trade Organization Protests, 2000. Interview by Monica Ghosh, May 2, 2000. 

**The interviewee in the excerpt above would most likely agree with which of the following claims?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Free trade leads to the exploitation of workers in developing nations.
Developing nations need multinational companies to create economic opportunities.
We should remove all obstacles to free trade including labor regulations.
Worldwide solidarity between workers is possible despite vastly different situations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Monica Ghosh: How and why did you get involved in the WTO mobilizations? 
>
>Tyree Scott: Well, for years now, [the Labor and Employment Law Office] has been involved with workers outside the United States, and in the last few years, we’ve come to understand the importance of this question of trade. And so we’ve actually, unlike a lot of the groups that came together just around this international stuff or the trade stuff or because of the WTO, we were already doing work around international trade and the relationship between workers in our country and workers abroad…
>
>In 1997, we had a meeting right here in Washington at Seabeck,1 over on the peninsula. That meeting was about bringing ordinary workers together to talk about this question of trade and how we related to it, and what it meant for workers in our country and workers abroad. What solidarity would mean, and what the current policies of both our government, the government of the G-7 countries, and the international lending institutions, what their policies represented. 
>
>So we were 35 workers from 11 countries. That’s what the discussion was about, like, how do we join this discussion about something as important as trade that affects our lives.

Source: Interview with Tyree Scott, World Trade Organization Protests, 2000. Interview by Monica Ghosh, May 2, 2000. 

**Which of the following best characterizes an ongoing impact of the developments described in the excerpt above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: International networks of labor organizers were created to work towards fairer trade practices.
Free trade practices were greatly reduced by the World Trade Organization.
International financial institutions have rejected market socialism.
The World Trade Organization promoted mercantilist ideology to compete for limited resources.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Article 1
>
>The Purposes of the United Nations are:
>
>1.To maintain international peace and security, and to that end: to take effective collective measures for the prevention and removal of threats to the peace, and for the suppression of acts of aggression or other breaches of the peace, and to bring about by peaceful means, and in conformity with the principles of justice and international law, adjustment or settlement of international disputes or situations which might lead to a breach of the peace;

Source: United Nations. Charter of the United Nations and Statute of the International Court of Justice. New York: United Nations, Office of Public Information, 1945. 


**Which of the following best characterizes the goal of the United Nations when it was established in 1945?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: collective security
protectionism
refugee rights
prosecute international aggressors

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Article I: Purposes
>
>The purposes of the Bank are:
>
>i. To assist in the reconstruction and development of territories of members by facilitating the investment of capital for productive purposes, including the restoration of economies destroyed or disrupted by war, the reconversion of productive facilities to peacetime needs and the encouragement of the development of productive facilities and resources in less developed countries.

Source: World Bank. “IBRD Articles of Agreement.” Washington, DC, 2012.  

**Which of the following best represents the way the goals of the organization described above have changed throughout the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They have increasingly emphasized economic liberalization.
They have increasingly opposed economic deregulation.
They have created mechanisms to weigh the input of all nations equally.
They have increasingly opposed climate adaptation and mitigation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements most accurately describes the difference between international non-governmental organizations (INGOs) and other international institutions?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: INGOs are typically focused on specific issues, while organizations like the UN handle a broader range of global issues.
INGOs are funded by member countries and have official governmental authority, unlike organizations like Amnesty International, Doctors without Borders, or Oxfam.
INGOs primarily provide loans and financial assistance to countries, whereas institutions like the World Bank focus on local economic development initiatives.
INGOs are specialized agencies of the UN that work on public health, unlike the World Health Organization (WHO) which is independent.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the broad historical context of the formation of the United Nations?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: decolonization and the formation of new states
the Cold War
the rise of empires
the fall of the Soviet Union
